articles.summary: A high-severity security flaw in Langflow, an open-source low-code platform to build artificial intelligence (AI) applications, has come under active exploitation in the wild, according to findings from VulnCheck. keypoints: Langflow path traversal enables unauthenticated arbitrary file writes
Active exploitation underway; CVSS 8.8 severity rating

analysis.title: Vulnerability mechanics
analysis.desc: Path traversal flaw in POST /api/v2 endpoint allows attackers to write files to arbitrary system locations without authentication.

analysis.title: Threat landscape
analysis.desc: VulnCheck reports active wild exploitation of CVE-2026-5027, indicating attackers already weaponizing vulnerability against Langflow deployments.

analysis.title: Risk exposure
analysis.desc: Unauthenticated access requirement means any internet-connected Langflow instance becomes immediate target for remote code execution attacks.

ai_summary.suggested_title: Langflow RCE Flaw CVE-2026-5027 Actively Exploited — Patch Now
ai_summary.bottom_line: If you're running Langflow, attackers are already trying to break in. Patch immediately or take it offline.
ai_summary.action_today: Langflow → Check version → Upgrade to patched release or disable /api/v2 endpoint until patched

articles.title: China-linked JDY botnet expands targeting of U.S. military networks
articles.summary: The JDY botnet, a malware network previously associated with Chinese threat actors like Volt Typhoon, has significantly expanded its targeting scope and reconnaissance efforts. [...]

articles.title: CISA Adds Cisco, Chrome, and Arista Flaws to KEV Catalog Amid Active Exploitation
articles.summary: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) on Tuesday added three new vulnerabilities to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) catalog, following reports of active exploitation. The list of vulnerabilities is as follows - CVE-2026-20245 (CVSS score: 7.8) - An improper encoding or escaping of output vulnerability in Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Manager that could allow an

keypoints: CISA added three actively exploited vulnerabilities to KEV catalog
Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Manager flaw allows unauthorized output manipulation

analysis.title: Active Exploitation Confirmed
analysis.desc: Three vulnerabilities added to CISA's KEV catalog indicate real-world attacks already underway against enterprise systems.

analysis.title: Cisco SD-WAN Manager at Risk
analysis.desc: CVE-2026-20245 (CVSS 7.8) enables improper encoding attacks on Catalyst SD-WAN Manager deployments without patching.

analysis.title: Multi-Vendor Impact
analysis.desc: Flaws affecting Cisco, Chrome, and Arista suggest coordinated vulnerability disclosure affecting critical infrastructure vendors.

articles.title: Who Runs the Ransomware Group ‘The Gentlemen?’
articles.summary: A cybercrime group known as The Gentlemen has emerged as the second most active ransomware gang by victim count, rapidly attracting a talented pool of hackers through an aggressive recruitment strategy that promises affiliates 90 percent of any ransom paid by victims. This post examines clues pointing to a real life identity for the administrator of The Gentlemen ransomware group.

keypoints: The Gentlemen ranks second most active ransomware gang globally
90 percent ransom split attracts elite hacker recruitment aggressively

analysis.title: Ransomware gang operational scale
analysis.desc: The Gentlemen has rapidly ascended to second position by victim count, indicating sophisticated infrastructure and effective targeting capabilities.

analysis.title: Aggressive affiliate recruitment model
analysis.desc: 90 percent ransom distribution to affiliates significantly exceeds industry standard rates, creating competitive advantage in talent acquisition.

analysis.title: Administrator identity investigation
analysis.desc: Article examines forensic clues attempting to unmask real-world identity of The Gentlemen's leadership structure and decision-makers.

ai_summary.suggested_title: Ransomware Gang The Gentlemen: Who Leads Them
ai_summary.bottom_line: The Gentlemen's 90 percent payouts mean they're actively recruiting your company's most vulnerable employees right now.
ai_summary.action_today: Check your email security logs for suspicious forwarding rules and authentication attempts from the past 30 days
